properties.name: Lower Manhattan
properties.description: The core of NYC, packed with iconic landmarks, nightlife, shopping, and some of the city’s best food.

properties.name: Midtown
properties.description: The heart of Manhattan, bright lights, major attractions, and high energy at all hours.

properties.name: Upper West Side
properties.description: Calm residential area with culture, and easy access to Central Park.

properties.name: Upper East Side
properties.description: Known for museums, shopping, and classic New York living.

properties.name: Central Park
properties.description: Manhattan’s iconic green space, perfect for walking, relaxing, and escaping the city without ever leaving it.

properties.name: Harlem
properties.description: Rich history, offers iconic music, food, and a strong neighborhood identity.

properties.name: Washington Heights / Inwood
properties.description: Lively, diverse, and home to amazing views, parks, and local culture.

properties.name: Western Queens
properties.description: Closest to Manhattan, this area blends modern waterfront development with vibrant, diverse neighborhoods and some of Queens’ best food scenes.

properties.name: Central Queens
properties.description: One of the most diverse areas in NYC, known for incredible international food, busy streets, and a strong everyday city vibe.

properties.name: Flushing Area
properties.description: A major cultural and commercial hub packed with world-class food, shopping, and nonstop activity.

properties.name: Eastern Queens
properties.description: More residential and suburban in feel, with quieter streets, parks, and a slower pace compared to the city core.

properties.name: Southern Queens / Rockaways
properties.description: A mix of residential neighborhoods and coastal areas, offering local community vibes and access to NYC’s beaches.
nta_names: Jacob Riis Park-Fort Tilden-Breezy Point Tip

properties.name: North Brooklyn
properties.description: Trendy with nightlife, cafes, and a strong creative scene.

properties.name: Northwest Brooklyn
properties.description: Historic and scenic, with some of the best views in the city.

properties.name: Central Brooklyn
properties.description: A mix of residential neighborhoods with parks, and everyday city life.

properties.name: South Brooklyn
properties.description: Laid back, with tree-lined streets, parks, and a strong neighborhood feel.

properties.name: Southern Brooklyn / Waterfront
properties.description: Home to beaches, boardwalks, and classic NYC seaside attractions.

properties.name: South Bronx
properties.description: The cultural heart of The Bronx, rich in history, music, and identity.

properties.name: Central Bronx
properties.description: Busy and energetic, with major attractions and dense local neighborhoods.

properties.name: North Bronx
properties.description: More open space, featuring large parks and some of NYC’s biggest attractions.

properties.name: West Bronx
properties.description: More residential, with elevated views and quieter neighborhoods.

properties.name: North Shore
properties.description: The most active part of the borough, closest to Manhattan with transit access and development.

properties.name: Central Staten Island
properties.description: Suburban and residential, with shopping areas and parks.

properties.name: South Shore
properties.description: The most suburban feeling area, with beaches, open space, and quiet neighborhoods.
